none: Light rail transit line and Banfield Hwy improvements, Multnomah County : environmental impact statement.

The Banfield Transitway Project proposed a light rail transit (LRT) line and improvements to the Banfield Freeway in Multnomah County, Oregon. The project aimed to address the expected increase in population and job growth in East Multnomah County and downtown Portland by 1990, which would lead to more commuter trips. The LRT system would connect downtown Portland with Gresham, improving transit service and reducing traffic congestion. The freeway improvements included widening and adding lanes to accommodate increased traffic. The project intended to support regional growth while minimizing environmental and community disruptions.
Light rail transit line and Banfield Hwy improvements, Multnomah County : environmental impact statement.

The Banfield Transitway Project proposed a light rail transit (LRT) line and improvements to the Banfield Freeway in Multnomah County, Oregon. The project aimed to address the expected increase in population and job growth in East Multnomah County and downtown Portland by 1990, which would lead to more commuter trips. The LRT system would connect downtown Portland with Gresham, improving transit service and reducing traffic congestion. The freeway improvements included widening and adding lanes to accommodate increased traffic. The project intended to support regional growth while minimizing environmental and community disruptions.

The Banfield Transitway Project was proposed to meet the anticipated rise in population and job opportunities in East Multnomah County and downtown Portland by 1990. There was some public concern regarding potential environmental impacts and community disruptions, but the project aimed to enhance transit options and reduce traffic congestion. Ultimately, the project was implemented as part of a broader effort to improve regional transportation infrastructure.
